title: Immunohistochemistry Protocol for Free-floating Fixed Tissue
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5j4vu","text":"Immunohistochemistry protocol for staining free-floating fixed tissue in the Kordower Laboratory.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"84uak","text":"HISTO- NOTES:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"et1sl","text":"Primate tissue staining dishes use  solution per dish","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":35,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"5g67s","text":"Rodent tissue staining dishes  solution per dish","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":30,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"7tm8t","text":"If staining a large number of primate cases, incubate 1' \u0026 2' Ab in individual cups to conserve volume of Ab used. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f2dvf","text":"Prepare bleach neutralizing solution prior to Step 12.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3p9g3","text":"Be conscious of tissue saturation while washing and incubating. i.e. Check that tissue is fully submerged in solution \u0026 not clumping. This will ensure proper penetration of antibodies \u0026 other reagents. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5ksfk","text":"Always include Positive \u0026 Negative Controls.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5oedh","text":"Positive: Use relevant control tissue to confirm specific antibody detection. (i.e. pS129; control tissue should consist of nigral sections previously successfully stained for pS129).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"63ep3","text":"Negative: Ideally, use tissue that you know does not contain the targeted antigen. If not available, use a section of tissue not incubated in the 1' Ab (primary delete).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4pvc1","text":"When incubating 1' Ab overnight, leave on shaker in refrigerator. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"99an7","text":"Can incubate in fridge on a shaker, covered in parafilm, over the weekend or up to 3 days.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7o48r","text":"Select a secondary antibody directed against the species in which the primary antibody was raised (i.e. if a primary antibody raised in rabbit is used, an anti-rabbit secondary antibody raised in a species other than rabbit must be used).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"amount","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"amount":"100","error":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"unit":2}},"1":{"type":"amount","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"amount":"50","error":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"unit":2}}}}

steps: Wash sections (6 x 00:10:00 ) in Dilution Media (DM) ( 0.2 Molarity (M) TBS plus 0.05 % volume Triton X-100).
Endogenous peroxidase inhibition ( 00:20:00 ). 0.1 Molarity (M) Sodium meta-periodate in TBS. 100 mL 0.2 Molarity (M) Tris-buffered saline (TBS) 2.13 g Sodium meta-periodate
Wash (2 x 00:10:00 ) in DM.
Serum blocking step ( 01:00:00 incubation): 100 mL DM 3 mL Normal Serum (species matching the host of the secondary antibody, e.g. horse, goat) 2 g Bovine Serum Albumin (BSA)

Incubation in primary antibody ( 18:00:00 - 72:00:00 ). See antibody catalog for concentration of primary antibody. 100 mL DM 1 mL Normal Serum (species matching the host of the secondary antibody, e.g. horse, goat) 1 g BSA 0.5 mL Triton X-100 **Optionally, refrigerate 4 °C to keep antibody stable**

Secondary antibody incubation ( 01:00:00 ) Concentration of secondary antibody is always 1:200 in solvent. 100 mL DM 1 mL Normal Serum (species matching the host of the secondary antibody, e.g. horse, goat) 1 g BSA
Wash (6 x 00:10:00 ) in DM. **(incubate ABC in solvent during these washes)**.
Avidin-Biotin Complex (ABC) Step ( 01:00:00 ) - Vectastain Elite ABC-HRP Kit (PK-6100). 100 mL DM 1 mL Normal Serum (species matching the host of the secondary antibody, e.g. horse, goat) 1 g BSA
Add ABC Reagent A and B to 1/10th of total desired volume of solvent.
Wash for 00:10:00 in DM.

Incubate for 00:30:00 at Room temperature . Then dilute 1:10 using the same solvent. This is your working solution. See chart below for example volumes. %s %s Working Solution A (drops) B (drops) 1/10th Working solution 25 mL 1 1 2.5 mL 50 mL 2 2 5 mL 100 mL 4 4 10 mL
Wash for 00:10:00 with TBS.

Wash (3 x 00:10:00 ) in 0.2 Molarity (M) Imidazole/ 1.0 Molarity (M) Sodium Acetate buffer pH 7.2 to pH 7.4 . 1000 mL dH 2 O 0.68 g Imidazole 6.8 g Sodium Acetate. Retain 100 mL of non-pH’d buffer for DAB preparation.
DAB Step (Neutralize DAB with bleach when done)
Wash developed tissue in imidazole acetate buffer (3 x 00:10:00 ). **Neutralize DAB with BLEACH!!!**
Wash (6 x 00:10:00 ) in DM.
Wash in DM for 00:10:00 (1/6).
Wash in DM for 00:10:00 (6/6).
Wash in DM for 00:10:00 (5/6).
Wash in DM for 00:10:00 (4/6).
Wash in DM for 00:10:00 (3/6).
Wash in DM for 00:10:00 (2/6).
Wash for 00:10:00 in DM (1/6).
Wash for 00:10:00 in DM (5/6).
Wash for 00:10:00 in DM (4/6).
Wash for 00:10:00 in DM (3/6).
Wash for 00:10:00 in DM (2/6).
Wash for 00:10:00 in DM (6/6).
Wash for 00:10:00 in DM (1/2).
Wash for 00:10:00 in DM (2/2).
Wash for 00:10:00 in Imidazole/Sodium Acetate buffer (1/3).
Wash for 00:10:00 in Imidazole/Sodium Acetate buffer (3/3).
Wash for 00:10:00 in Imidazole/Sodium Acetate buffer (2/3).
Wash sections for 00:10:00 in DM (1/6).
Wash sections for 00:10:00 in DM (6/6).
Wash sections for 00:10:00 in DM (5/6).
Wash sections for 00:10:00 in DM (4/6).
Wash sections for 00:10:00 in DM (3/6).
Wash sections for 00:10:00 in DM (2/6).

Make DAB solution 100 mL non-pH'd imidazole acetate buffer from above 50 mg 3,3-Diaminobenzidine Tetrahydrochloride (DAB) 2 g Nickel(II) sulfate hexahydrate **(Only used with certain primary antibodies, chromagen enhancer that changes brown DAB precipitate to blue-purple)**

To monitor signal, move all tissue to imidazole buffer, remove one section and mount on an UNSUBBED slide and view under microscope. Place all tissue back in DAB solution to increase signal intensity, if needed.
Place tissue in DAB solution. Develop tissue for approximately 00:04:00 to 00:07:00 . Timing is critical, ensure all tissue spends the same amount of time in DAB solution.
Start reaction -- add 500 µL of 1 % (v/v) hydrogen peroxide (H 2 O 2 ) to the above DAB mixture just prior to use. OR add 16.7 µL of 30 % (v/v) hydrogen peroxide (H 2 O 2 ), per 100 mL .
Make 1 % (v/v) Hydrogen Peroxide (H 2 O 2 ) 3 mL of dH 2 O 100 µL of 30 % (v/v) hydrogen peroxide (H 2 O 2 )
Wash developed tissue in imidazole acetate buffer for 00:10:00 (1/3).
Wash developed tissue in imidazole acetate buffer for 00:10:00 (2/3).
Wash developed tissue in imidazole acetate buffer for 00:10:00 (3/3).
Store tissue in 0.2 Molarity (M) Phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) in refrigerator 4 °C until mounted on slides.
creator: Scott Muller
